name: Quiet Days along the Dessarin
author: Leaontar Dlarvin
description:
Leaontar Dlarvin records slow seasons on the river — empty quays, gossip in riverside taverns, and the small rituals that keep bargemen sane between cargoes.

The prose is gentle and observational: herons in the reeds, children fishing off piers, and the particular silence that falls when winter ice begins to creep south from the Surbrin.

name: Planar Travels: A Study of the Planes of Good
author: Elminster
description:
Planar Travels: A Study of the Planes of Good — A treatise on magic as it is actually practiced — wards, reputations, and the price of mispronunciation. Elminster is credited in library catalogues; the work is dated to before 1371 DR.

Complete copies are rare; excerpted versions circulate among mages who insist the missing pages do not matter.
